audit_items.proof_trace: Proof fails at two sites in get_modlist: (1) calloc at L199 is not checked before bvals[i] is written at L202, so OOM causes an immediate NULL dereference; (2) malloc at L202 is not checked before bvals[i]->bv_val is written at L215-216, another NULL dereference. get_list has the same pattern: malloc/realloc at L88-91 are not checked before result[num++] at L93. The print_search_entry concern (ldap_get_dn NULL at L754) is mitigated because ldap_dn2ufn handles NULL safely (getdn.c:371-373).
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker who can exhaust process memory (e.g., via ulimit or resource pressure on the host running the test binary) triggers calloc/malloc failure in get_modlist or get_list, causing a NULL pointer dereference and crash; however, this is a local interactive CLI test utility, not a network-accessible server component, so direct external triggering is not straightforward.
